title: -Contract: Bay Cities Paving & Grading, Inc. for FY 2025 Street Maintenance and Rehabilitation Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Approving plans and specifications for the FY 2025 Street Maintenance and
Rehabilitation Project, Specification No. 25-11690-C;
2. Accepting the bid of Bay Cities Paving & Grading, Inc. as the lowest, responsive and
responsible bidder; and
3. Authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any amendments, extensions
or other change orders until completion of the project, in accordance with the
approved plans and specifications in an amount not to exceed $12,323,848.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding is available as listed below. No other funding is required, and no other projects will
be delayed due to this expenditure. This project is funded through the existing Five-Year
Paving Plan, as adopted by Council in 2023. Revenue from the 2024 voter approved
Sustainable and Accountable Funding for Equitable Street Transformation, Revitalization,
Enhancements, and Essential Traffic Safety (SAFE STREETS) Initiative Parcel Tax (Measure
FF) will not be available until December of 2025.

sections.current_situation:
The FY 2025 Street Maintenance and Rehabilitation Project (Specification No. 25-11690-C)
was released for bids on February 13, 2025, and bids were opened on March 13, 2025. Two
non-local bids were received, from a low of $11,203,498.17 to a high of $12,978,378.75 (see
Attachment 3, Abstract of Bids).
The low bidder was Bay Cities Paving & Grading, Inc., with a bid of $11,203,498.17, which is
below the Engineer’s estimate of $13.2 million. Staff has verified that Bay Cities Paving &
Grading, Inc. is the lowest, responsive and responsible bidder. This project includes a 10%
contingency, which brings the total contract amount up to the requested $12,323,848. Staff
recommends that a contract for this project be awarded to Bay Cities Paving & Grading, Inc.
The FY 2025 Street Maintenance and Rehabilitation Project is a Strategic Plan Priority
Project, advancing our goal of providing state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure,
amenities, and facilities.
sections.background:
Several streets throughout Berkeley are scheduled for rehabilitation as part of the City’s Five-
year Street Rehabilitation Plan (Five-Year Plan). The City maintains a rolling Five-Year Plan
for resurfacing and repaving City streets. Staff develops the plan using a Pavement
Management System supported by the Metropolitan Transportation Commission (MTC). The
Five-Year Plan was updated for Fiscal Years 2024 through 2028 by Resolution 71,120-N.S.
adopted by Council on November 28, 2023. The Five-Year Plan aims to complete 46.73 miles
of street rehabilitation with nearly $90 million in funding over five years. Funding sources
primarily include the State Transportation Tax, Road Repair and Accountability Act, and
Measure BB. Additionally, a new annual allocation of $1 million from the Zero Waste Fund for
FY 2024 to FY 2026, increasing to $2 million for FY 2027 and FY 2028, is intended to offset
the impact of Zero Waste collection vehicles on the City’s pavement.
The Street Rehabilitation and Repair Policy created by Resolution 55,384-N.S. and most
recently updated by 70,204-N.S. to include Equity Zone considerations, contains the essential
criteria for developing the plan and consists of the following:
 To the extent financially practical, implementing the paving plan shall advance
California Assembly Bill 413 (AB 413) and City plans, e.g., the Complete Streets
Policy, Vision Zero Policy and Action Plan, Pedestrian Plan, and Bicycle Plan.
 Rehabilitation of contiguous sections of roadway rather than one block at a time shall
be preferred when feasible.
 Strive to maintain all roads within the primary transportation network (arterials,
collectors, bus routes, existing and proposed low-stress bikeway network, and equity
zone) at a target Pavement Conditions Index (PCI) of 70.
 The benefits of good infrastructure shall be distributed equitably throughout the entire
community regardless of the income, political influence, or demographic
characteristics of the residents o
sections.rationale:
This street repair project is part of the City’s annual program to rehabilitate deteriorated
streets. The work requires contracted services, as the City does not possess the in-house
labor or equipment resources necessary to complete it.
sections.alternatives:
No alternative actions were considered for this pavement rehabilitation project. The City
could choose not to proceed with it. However, the street pavement would then continue to
deteriorate, drainage improvements would not be made, and Complete Streets, Vision Zero
and bicycle and pedestrian elements would not be installed, affecting the community's and
others' use of the streets.

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: BAY CITIES PAVING & GRADING, INC. FOR
FY2025 STREET MAINTENANCE AND REHABILITATION PROJECT
WHEREAS, The FY 2025 Street Maintenance and Rehabilitation Project is part of the
City’s ongoing Street Capital Improvement Program to rehabilitate deteriorated streets
located throughout the City; and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the labor nor the equipment necessary to undertake this
FY 2025 Street Maintenance and Rehabilitation Project; and
WHEREAS, an invitation for bids was duly advertised, and Bay Cities Paving & Grading,
Inc. was the lowest responsive and responsible bidder; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in the FY 2025 budget in the Street Capital Improvement
Program State Transportation Tax (Fund 127), Road Maintenance & Rehabilitation
Account (127-SB1), Measure B Sales Tax (Fund 130), Measure F Vehicle Registration
Fee (Fund 133), Measure BB Sales Tax (Fund 134), Capital Improvement Fund (Fund
501), Zero Waste Fund (Fund 601), and Clean Storm Fund (Fund 616).
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Plans and Specification No. 25-11690-C for the FY 2025 Street Maintenance and
Rehabilitation Project are approved.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley authorizes the City
Manager to execute a contract and any amendments, extensions, or change orders until
completion of the project in accordance with the approved plans and specifications with
Bay Cities Paving & Grading, Inc. for the FY 2025 Street Maintenance and Rehabilitation
Project, in an amount not to exceed $12,323,848, which includes a 10% contingency for
unforeseen circumstances. A record signature copy of said agreement and any
amendments will be on file in the Office of the City Clerk.
Page 6 of 7
FY 2025 Street Maintenance & Rehabilitation Project
